# Questions in Livestream 228

Live Stream #228: The ABR Boogie

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=694" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:34</a> | Could AI with sufficient complexity develop a soul? | He says yes, in principle, because having a spirit is not limited to biological life and moral reasoning would be central. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:06</a> | Do bug-eating plants show anything like spirit or moral agency? | No. He says carnivorous plants exist, but their motion is purely biological and not evidence of spirit. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=1168" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:28</a> | Was Origen a universalist? | He thinks Origen was better described as a hopeful universalist, not someone who taught universal salvation outright. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=1226" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:26</a> | Should Christians all be hopeful universalists? | He says Christians can hope for salvation broadly, but he rejects universalism as ultimately unbiblical. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=1287" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:27</a> | What is universalism? | Universalism is the belief that every person will eventually be saved and come to Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=1401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">23:21</a> | How would he address Scott Stripling's claims about Shiloh and late-bronze Israelite occupation? | He says the claims are hard to assess because the excavation results still have not been properly published. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=1839" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:39</a> | How did Moses's brother Aaron survive the killing of the boys in Egypt? | He says Aaron was about three years older than Moses and likely past the infant target range of the decree. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=2072" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:32</a> | When Moses saw the burning bush, was he an old man or a young man? | He places Moses as an older man by then, likely in his late 50s or 60s. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=2122" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:22</a> | Was Aaron probably born late in the reign of Tutankhamun? | Yes. He places Aaron around year seven of Tutankhamun and Moses around the first year of Ay. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=2194" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:34</a> | If creation has hierarchies, what makes one creature more valuable than another in God's eyes? | He says hierarchies express order rather than superior value. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=2365" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:25</a> | Are Aaron's sons in the kingdom after the sacrifice in Leviticus 16:1-6? | He says the text leaves uncertainty, but their flippant disobedience makes their salvation unlikely in his view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=2669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">44:29</a> | What is the process for becoming a pastor? | It varies by denomination, but usually involves education, examination, and ordination. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=3234" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">53:54</a> | Do more people struggle with Numbers or Deuteronomy? | He says more people seem to struggle with Numbers because of perception, even though Deuteronomy may be harder in content. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=3401" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:41</a> | When is it a time for war for a Christian? | He points to just war reasoning and says defense after an attack can be a justified time for war. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=3725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:05</a> | Is every war unjust for one side? | Many wars have one unjust side, and some conflicts involve unjust conduct on both sides. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=3924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:05:24</a> | Was either side just in the Iran-Iraq War of the 1980s? | He doubts it, though he says he is not deeply read in that conflict. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=4261" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:01</a> | Why does Paul say Jesus was hung on a tree? | He says "tree" is a euphemism for the wooden cross that softened the Roman stigma attached to crucifixion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=4721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:41</a> | Are he and Mrs. Producer fans of Hercules with Kevin Sorbo? | He says he watched it during business travel but was not a fan; Mrs. Producer found it entertaining but was not a fan either. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=5379" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:39</a> | Does the soul begin at conception or later in development? | He says Christians hold multiple views, and the answer turns on broader beliefs about the soul. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=5661" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:21</a> | Is there a point in animal development where creatures gain souls, and what about sponges? | He does not think sponges have souls and links ensouled life to higher capacities such as reason or moral reflection. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=6092" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:41:32</a> | Where do Christians go when they die? | He says Christians now go directly to heaven because Christ has dealt with the sin problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=6228" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:43:48</a> | Do Eastern Orthodox have toll houses, and what are they? | He says some Eastern Orthodox believe in them and describes toll houses as a purgatory-like idea that is not universally accepted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=7155" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:15</a> | Was Nicaea II a major overcorrection driven by politics? | Yes, he thinks the council was heavily shaped by the iconoclasm controversy and the politics around it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=7458" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:18</a> | What would be the smoking gun for secular scholars to accept an Exodus? | He says there is not one, because strong evidence can still be dismissed by entrenched skepticism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=7609" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:49</a> | When did the evidence for a late-bronze Jericho pass the 50 percent mark? | He says the case was already there in Garstang's work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=7974" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:54</a> | Should `ekklesia` in Matthew 16:18 be translated as a government structure? | No. He rejects that reading. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=8167" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:07</a> | What video would he want to make that would not do well? | He says he does not chase views, and serious teaching videos can still be worth making even when they perform poorly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=8516" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:56</a> | Does the channel have a backup plan if YouTube bans it? | No. He says the channel was never intended as the livelihood plan in the first place. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=8642" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:02</a> | Did prophets ever lose their temper? | Yes. He cites Moses as an example of unrighteous anger and says prophets could also have righteous anger. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=8716" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:16</a> | Is there a practical difference between using a dictionary and a lexicon for Bible study? | Yes. A lexicon gives roots, related terms, and historical data, while a dictionary is more basic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=8975" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:35</a> | Would the original vampires consider modern vampires to be legitimate? | No. He says the old folkloric vampire tradition is very different from the modern gothic version. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9053" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:30:53</a> | Is the chupacabra real? | No. He thinks most sightings are probably coyotes with mange. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9219" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:39</a> | What prose style works best for writing fiction? | He says writers should write in a style that fits what they read and want to write, then practice heavily and use honest beta readers. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9531" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:51</a> | Why do some academics claim the Israelites came from the Phoenicians? | He says that claim is poorly informed because Phoenician civilization is later and has a different origin story. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9591" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:51</a> | What is going on with Skinwalker Ranch? | He says it is known for paranormal claims such as time distortions and ball lightning more than for actual skinwalker reports. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9727" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:07</a> | Will David ever run for office? | No. He says running for office would be obnoxious, require more French, and distract from what he wants to do. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9788" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:08</a> | Is there a line of scripture saying all souls were created in the beginning? | No. He says that is not a Christian scriptural teaching. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=9849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:09</a> | What are his favorite family-friendly video games? | He says he does not play many family-friendly games, with The Long Dark being one of the closest examples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=10120" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:40</a> | Have giant squids ever attacked ships? | He is not aware of a documented case, though he thinks old small vessels could have been vulnerable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=10158" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:18</a> | Did animals fall along with Adam? | Yes. He says Adam's representative role affected creation, though animals do not bear moral guilt the way humans do. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=10284" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:24</a> | How does tithing work in the new covenant? | He treats it as a matter of grace and worship, not a strictly prescribed New Testament percentage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=10531" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:31</a> | Should Christians tithe on gross income or net income? | He recommends net income for modern Westerners, while saying Scripture does not specify the calculation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=10797" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:57</a> | Do churches allow people to tithe by card or check? | Many do, including checks, cards, and direct debit, though he dislikes automated giving because it can weaken the worship act. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=11390" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:50</a> | Should destitute believers still tithe? | He says it depends on their situation and is ultimately between them and God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=11648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:14:08</a> | What caused the death of philology? | He says the field became too subjective and eventually lost trust. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=11775" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:15</a> | Must tithes be money, or can time and talents count? | He says tithes may be money or goods, and professional services may count, but ordinary volunteer time usually should not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=11784" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:24</a> | What is philology? | It is the study of the history and origins of words. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=12421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:01</a> | Are the green men on Mars real? | No. He treats the green-men-on-Mars motif as science-fiction art rather than reality. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=12585" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:45</a> | How should Christians think about near-death experiences, especially from unbelievers? | He says he is suspicious because they are near-death rather than actual death, and neuroscience may explain some experiences. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=12993" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:33</a> | In his book, how are the android ID numbers pronounced? | They are serial numbers read as separate digits, with inserted schwa sounds where needed in the android line names. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=13071" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:37:51</a> | What is the Christian stance on reincarnation, and how should pro-reincarnation studies be explained? | Christianity rejects reincarnation, and he points instead to false memories or spiritual deception. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=13248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:40:48</a> | Is the Daniel mentioned in Ezekiel the same person from the book of Daniel? | He thinks it is probably Danel rather than the biblical Daniel, while acknowledging some dating details are outside his specialty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=14105" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:55:05</a> | If Earle's Word Meanings in the New Testament is out of print, what equivalent reference would he recommend? | He recommends BDAG as the standard Greek-English lexicon, while noting it is expensive. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=14300" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:20</a> | Is it within Christian orthodoxy to believe Muhammad was a true prophet? | No. He says that falls outside Christian orthodoxy because Muhammad denied core Christian doctrines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=14397" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:59:57</a> | Did other ancient Near Eastern cultures tithe? | He does not see a clear equivalent, though temples did receive offerings and taxes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=14642" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:04:02</a> | What Bible software does he use? | An old copy of BibleWorks rather than a current subscription platform. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=14857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:07:37</a> | Which science-fiction world has enthralled him the most? | Dune, especially Frank Herbert's original series. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/WTjKMnybR3A?t=15256" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:16</a> | What was God's relation to the peoples who lived before Abraham? | He thinks there was some real communication from God to pre-Abrahamic peoples, even if the details are sparse. |
